news.title: RC Fornax, Bango, Quantum Blockchain, Seraphim Space Investment Trust - Small Cap Snapshot
news.summary: RC Fornax (AIM:RCFX) has been selected by Thales UK for its sonar and naval architecture supplier framework, in a deal that could run for up to five years. Thales has historically accounted for around 30% of RC Fornax's revenue. Shares in Bango Bango PLC (AIM:BGO, OTCQX:BGOPF) soared after the company reported strong first-half results, with annual recurring revenue up 31% to $20.4 million. The company remains on track to meet full-year expectations, with adjusted EBITDA expected to rise 34%. Quantum Blockchain Technologies (AIM:QBT) says its R&D team has developed a new mathematical variant of Method B for Bitcoin mining using chaos mathematics. Live testing has begun on a Bitaxe Gamma miner, with further tests on an ASIC manufacturer's development kit to follow. Seraphim Space Investment Trust (LSE:SSIT) has completed the sale of portfolio company ALL.SPACE to York Space Systems, but the deal's value has come in below the last reported carrying value. The shortfall is down to York's share price falling below the agreed issue price for the equity component. Follow us and subscribe on YouTube, our social channels, and on proactiveinvestors.co.uk.

news.title: Stock Futures Rebound as Markets Shrug Off U.S.-Iran Strikes
news.summary: Stocks looked set to bounce back as investors appeared to get over the worst of their worries about tensions between the U.S. and Iran, even after the two sides traded fire again on Wednesday night.  Dow Jones Industrial Average futures climbed 47 points, or 0.1%, on Thursday, S&P 500 futures added 0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures gained 0.8%.  President Donald Trump sparked the rally in crude when he said the cease-fire between the U.S. and Iran was “over.”

news.title: Crude pares steep gains as traders take stock after US-Iran flare-up
news.summary: Oil prices fell Thursday, clawing back some of the previous day's surge, as investors assessed the outlook for Middle East peace talks after the US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes and Donald Trump said their ceasefire was over.Trump ordered new strikes and warned of "much worse" if Tehran continues to attack vessels in the strait, through which a fifth of world oil usually passes.
